feedback.text: I would like to put forward that the Digital Fairness Act should also include safeguards for consumers owning digital services that would allow them to keep these services in a usable manner to at least some reasonable degree. Products that were sold digitally or even as physical copies but with digital elements should not be rendered unusable at the behest of one party without any workarounds, as it would be the same as taking away a product after purchase. I believe that the suggestions from the Stop Killing Games initiative would be beneficial in strengthening consumer rights and introducing transparency into a legally very murky environment. I also believe it would be important to consider protection for businesses from third parties dictating the nature and details of their sold products as long they adhere to the category of products sold. As an example, I am very concerned about Visa, MasterCard and other payment processing companies being able to dictate to platforms like Steam or itch.io which specific products can be sold on their platforms. This is a move that is not just inherently anti-consumer, but also damages the industry as a whole. Thank you for your time and consideration
